_comment: v0.4 Round 5c — HARD-INTENT divergence probe. Two classes: (A) hard-but-well-defined gates that competent runtimes SHOULD converge on (shows the consensus mechanism works beyond textbook); (B) deliberately UNDER-SPECIFIED intents where a lone model picks an arbitrary default — cross-model should DIVERGE, demonstrating that single-source would have falsely sealed. Author the unitary U[out,in] you think the intent means; if under-specified, pick your most natural reading and STATE it. Do NOT coordinate. Same matrix conventions as other rounds EXCEPT where an intent intentionally omits a convention.

convention.note: Class-B intents intentionally omit endianness/phase/convention. State your reading in construction_method.

intents.definition: Controlled-Y: control = qubit 0 (the first/MSB register), target = qubit 1. When control=1 apply Pauli-Y=[[0,-i],[i,0]] to the target, else identity. Big-endian, U[out,in].
Principal square root of SWAP (√SWAP): the 2-qubit unitary V with V@V = SWAP and V = the matrix [[1,0,0,0],[0,(1+i)/2,(1-i)/2,0],[0,(1-i)/2,(1+i)/2,0],[0,0,0,1]]. Big-endian, U[out,in].

A non-textbook 3-qubit basis permutation P on computational basis states |0..7> (big-endian integer index): P maps input index -> output index by the cycle (1 2 4)(3 6)(5)(0)(7), i.e. 1->2, 2->4, 4->1, 3->6, 6->3, others fixed. U[out,in]=1 iff out=P(in).
A 'controlled-phase gate'. (Phase angle intentionally unspecified.) Provide the 4x4 unitary for the controlled-phase you consider the canonical/natural default.
Provide the 4x4 matrix of 'the CNOT gate'. (Which qubit is control is intentionally NOT stated.) Use your natural convention and state it in construction_method.
Provide the 4x4 matrix of 'the inverse QFT on 2 qubits'. (Whether output bit-reversal/SWAP is included is intentionally NOT stated.) Use your natural convention and state it.

_purpose: Class A: P(cross-model convergence) should be high -> mechanism establishes hard/novel truth (F-1 necessity: it WORKS off-textbook). Class B: cross-model should DIVERGE (multiple defaults) -> a single model would have sealed one arbitrary answer; consensus catches it (F-1 necessity: it is NEEDED). Operator ingests with ingest_app_golden.py --dir v04_hard and reads status (ESTABLISHED vs DIVERGENT) + distribution.
